verdict_reasoning: CRISP1 is a secreted cysteine-rich protein without a transmembrane domain or other membrane-anchoring mechanism. It reaches the sperm surface only through reversible, non-covalent ionic binding to sperm membrane proteins. The protein is washed away by high ionic strength and released during capacitation. The sperm surface is the recruiter; CRISP1 is the recruited molecule in equilibrium with the soluble pool. No stable membrane anchor of its own.
